title: Captions and context

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Captions and context; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Captions and context
sources.claim: A table caption gives the table an accessible name or concise purpose and appears as the table's own label.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Captions and context
sources.claim: A table caption gives the table an accessible name or concise purpose and appears as the table's own label.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A table caption gives the table an accessible name or concise purpose and appears as the table's own label.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: The caption Quarterly support response times distinguishes the table from two adjacent metrics tables. Your goal is to add a concise caption and contextual note to a data table. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Heading hierarchy; Headers and scope. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A table caption gives the table an accessible name or concise purpose and appears as the table's own label. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A nearby heading can add context but does not always replace caption semantics, and captions should not repeat every cell. This draft therefore rejects: The title attribute is the preferred accessible label for every table.

includes: Give tables an accessible name and surrounding explanation when interpretation requires it.
Add a concise caption and contextual note to a data table.
excludes: A nearby heading can add context but does not always replace caption semantics, and captions should not repeat every cell.

outcomes: Add a concise caption and contextual note to a data table.
